mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single quarter, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances following the threshold breach, or the predictive hazard ratio drops below 1.0 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while consumer credit outstanding rises indicates inventory destocking amid persistent demand, which historically precedes a recessionary earnings contraction and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline + RSAFS increase) shows no statistically significant negative correlation with forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) across three full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening where the federal funds rate increases by more than 50 basis points in consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand sharply, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in over 55 percent of tightening episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50 percent in a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDP growth relative to forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 for three consecutive years, or forward returns show no mean reversion after extreme GDP deviations.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 98.0 for two consecutive months, it indicates a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below 98.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns average positive values during consecutive INDPRO declines below 98.0, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5 percent in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in new housing starts signals a synchronized breakdown in both labor demand and business investment, which historically triggers a sharp equity valuation reset and negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling HOUST does not produce a statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.05) across two full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation regimes where CPI exceeds 5.0 percent annually, real wage growth turns negative and consumer spending power erodes, causing demand-driven equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of observations when CPI exceeds 5.0 percent, or the inflation-return correlation flips sign during stagflation periods.
